zh.lifeSummary: 當john le childe於大約1360年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾諾維其可時，他的父親thomas le childe30歲，母親Mrs Thomas le Childe27歲。他於1385年與de Clipton heiress在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1426年在英國武斯特斯爾諾維其可享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾諾維其可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当john le childe于大约1360年出生在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾諾維其可时，他的父亲thomas le childe30岁，母亲Mrs Thomas le Childe27岁。他于1385年与de Clipton heiress在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾伍斯特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1426年在英国武斯特斯爾諾維其可享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾諾維其可。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: childe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Child
meanings: nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: john
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When john le childe was born about 1360, in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, thomas le childe, was 30 and his mother, Mrs Thomas le Childe, was 27. He married de Clipton heiress in 1385, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1426, in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque john le childe est né environ 1360, à Northwick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, thomas le childe, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs Thomas le Childe, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé de Clipton heiress en 1385, à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1426, à Northwick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Northwick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als john le childe um 1360 in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater thomas le childe 30 und seine Mutter Mrs Thomas le Childe 27. Er hat 1385 in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom de Clipton heiress geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1426 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Northwick, Worcestershire, England und wurde in Northwick, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando john le childe è nato il circa 1360 nel Northwick, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, thomas le childe, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Thomas le Childe, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato de Clipton heiress nel 1385, nel Worcester, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1426, nel Northwick, Worcestershire, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Northwick, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: john le childeが約1360年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアノースウィックで生まれた時，父thomas le childeは30歳，母Mrs Thomas le Childeは27歳であった。1385年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターでde Clipton heiressと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1426年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアノースウィックで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアノースウィックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: john le childe(이)가 대략 1360년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 노르스윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 thomas le childe(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Thomas le Childe(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1385년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 de Clipton heiress(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1426년 영국 워스스터실르 노르스윅에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 노르스윅에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando john le childe nasceu aproximadamente 1360, em Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, thomas le childe, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Thomas le Childe, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com de Clipton heiress em 1385, em Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1426, em Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда john le childe родился около 1360 г. в местности нортвикк, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени thomas le childe было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Thomas le Childe, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени de Clipton heiress в 1385 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1426 г. в местности нортвикк, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности нортвикк, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando john le childe nació aproximadamente 1360, en Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, thomas le childe, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Mrs Thomas le Childe, tenía 27 años. Se casó con de Clipton heiress en 1385, en Worcester, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1426, en Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Northwick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
